subject: Databases
title: Missing Indexes
description: A quick, practical guide to Missing Indexes.

hook.text: A WHERE clause on email scans 5 million rows. Add an index on email: same query, 3 milliseconds.

buildup.text: Indexes are the single biggest lever for query performance. Missing indexes are the most common cause of slow queries.

discovery.text: pg_stat_user_tables shows sequential scans per table. High seq_scan counts mean missing indexes.

twist.text: Developers add columns to WHERE clauses without adding indexes. The slow query appears weeks later under load.

climax.text: Review every WHERE, JOIN ON, and ORDER BY column. If it's selective, it likely needs an index.

punchline.text: Missing indexes: the most common and fixable performance problem.

quiz.question: How can you detect missing indexes in PostgreSQL?
options: Check pg_stat_user_tables for tables with high sequential scan counts
Missing indexes cause the database to crash
The database automatically adds all needed indexes
